bill.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText: [Congressional Record Volume 163, Number 202 (Tuesday, December 12, 2017)][Pages H9845-H9846]From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]By Ms. KUSTER of New Hampshire:H.R. 4634.Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuantto the following:Article I Section 8, Clause 4 of the United StatesConstitution, the Naturalization Clause: ``The Congress shallhave Power To establish an uniform Rule of Naturaliza-tion. . . .''[[Page H9846]]____________________

item.name: Immigration Integrity, Security, and Enforcement Subcommittee
Immigration Integrity, Security, and Enforcement Subcommittee

item.text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Immigration and Border Security.
Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

summary.text: Provides for the relief of Arpita Kurdekar, Girish Kurdekar, and Vandana Kurdekar.

bill.title: For the relief of Arpita Kurdekar, Girish Kurdekar, and Vandana Kurdekar.

latestAction.text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Immigration and Border Security.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
